prompt: WPA Federal Art Project poster, 1930s New Deal lithograph. A scientist or worker figure standing beside a large chart, gesturing toward an upward-trending scatter plot with a bold diagonal best-fit line cutting through clusters of data points. Graph axes labeled. Small secondary charts and data in background. Dense, layered composition filling the entire frame. Color palette: deep teal, brick red, warm cream, dark navy. All flat unmodulated fills, no gradients, no shading, no photography. Bold geometric sans-serif text at top: PREDICT THE FUTURE. Smaller text at bottom: LINEAR REGRESSION — WORKS PROGRESS ADMINISTRATION. Strong silhouettes, lithograph print texture, slight ink wear on edges. Every inch of the poster filled with purposeful design elements. No empty space.
tradition_approach: Flat unmodulated color fills, bold sans-serif typography, limited 3-color palette, strong figure-ground contrast, and simplified geometric shapes characteristic of New Deal lithograph printing constraints.
